PolyQ package¶

Submodules¶

PolyQ.engine module¶

This file does not contain the functions needed for universal simulation. It only includes the funcitons needed for simulating the Clifford + S + T gates and their inverses. By Clifford, we mean {H,Z,CZ,CCZ} set here.

This file contain all the essential functions required for each step from creating the polynomial as an array, to returning the final state vector after calculating the truth table for each possible values of variables.

PolyQ.engine.create_poly(qc, n)¶
PolyQ.engine.eval_f(terms, x, n)¶
PolyQ.engine.eval_f_no_ivs(terms, x, n)¶
PolyQ.engine.get_statevector(ttb, n, t, ovs, starting_index=0)¶
PolyQ.engine.get_statevector_file(ttb, n, t, ovs, starting_index=0)¶
PolyQ.engine.get_truthtable(terms, n, t, initial_state)¶
PolyQ.engine.get_truthtable_no_ivs(terms, n, t, initial_state)¶

PolyQ.simulation module¶

PolyQ.simulation.simulate(qc, initial_state=0)¶

Simulates a quantum circuit from a given initial state.

Parameters:

  • qc (QuantumCircuit) – Quantum circuit to be simulated.

  • initial_state (int | str | list[int]) – Initial state of the qubits.

Returns:

The resulting statevector.

Return type:

list[complex]

Module contents¶

PolyQ.simulate(qc, initial_state=0)¶

Simulates a quantum circuit from a given initial state.

Parameters:

  • qc (QuantumCircuit) – Quantum circuit to be simulated.

  • initial_state (int | str | list[int]) – Initial state of the qubits.

Returns:

The resulting statevector.

Return type:

list[complex]
